args.command: mkdir -p /workspace/research /workspace/decisions
curl -sL "https://1password.com/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | head -5000 > /tmp/1password_pricing.html && wc -c /tmp/1password_pricing.html
curl -sL "https://bitwarden.com/product/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | head -5000 > /tmp/bitwarden_pricing.html && wc -c /tmp/bitwarden_pricing.html
curl -sL "https://www.dashlane.com/pricing" 2>/dev/null | head -5000 > /tmp/dashlane_pricing.html && wc -c /tmp/dashlane_pricing.html
curl -sL "https://www.keeper.com/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | head -5000 > /tmp/keeper_pricing.html && wc -c /tmp/keeper_pricing.html
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/features/business" 2>/dev/null | head -5000 > /tmp/lastpass_pricing.html && wc -c /tmp/lastpass_pricing.html
grep -i -E "(price|per user|per seat|monthly|annual|\$[0-9])" /tmp/1password_pricing.html | head -40
curl -sL "https://bitwarden.com/product/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | grep -i -E "(price|per user|per seat|monthly|annual|\$[0-9])" | head -40
curl -sL "https://www.dashlane.com/pricing" 2>/dev/null | grep -i -E "(price|per user|per seat|monthly|annual|\$[0-9])" | head -40
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/features/business" 2>/dev/null | grep -i -E "(price|per user|per seat|monthly|annual|\$[0-9])" | head -40

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re
from html.parser import HTMLParser

# Parse 1Password pricing
with open('/tmp/1password_pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Look for pricing numbers
prices = re.findall(r'\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?', content)
print("1Password prices found:", set(prices))

# Look for tier names
tiers = re.findall(r'(Teams|Business|Enterprise|Family|Individual|Personal)', content)
print("1Password tiers found:", set(tiers))

# Look for per user mentions
per_user = re.findall(r'.{0,50}per.{0,50}user.{0,50}', content, re.IGNORECASE)
for p in per_user[:10]:
    print("  per user:", p.strip()[:120])
PYEOF

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re

with open('/tmp/1password_pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Extract JSON-like pricing data
pricing_blocks = re.findall(r'priceAnnual["\']:\s*["\']\$[\d.]+["\']', content)
print("Annual pricing blocks:", pricing_blocks[:20])

# Find Teams tier pricing
teams = re.findall(r'priceAnnual["\']:\s*["\']\$(\d+\.\d+)["\']', content)
print("All annual prices:", teams)

# Find the Teams section
teams_section = re.findall(r'.{0,200}Teams.{0,200}priceAnnual.{0,200}', content)
for s in teams_section[:3]:
    print("Teams section:", s[:300])
PYEOF

curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/features/business" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, re
content = sys.stdin.read()
# Look for pricing text
text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', content)
text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text)
# Find pricing mentions
for m in re.finditer(r'.{0,100}\$[\d,]+.{0,100}', text):
    print(m.group(0).strip())
" | head -30

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re, json

with open('/tmp/1password_pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Find all pricing detail blocks with tier names
blocks = re.findall(r'entryTitle["\']:\s*["\']PWM Pricing - (\w+) USD["\'].*?priceAnnual["\']:\s*["\']\$(\d+\.\d+)["\'].*?priceMonthly["\']:\s*["\']\$(\d+\.\d+)["\']', content)
for tier, annual, monthly in blocks:
    print(f"  {tier}: ${annual}/mo annual, ${monthly}/mo monthly")

# Also find Business tier
blocks2 = re.findall(r'entryTitle["\']:\s*["\']PWM Pricing - (\w+) USD["\'].*?priceAnnual["\']:\s*["\']\$(\d+\.\d+)["\']', content)
for tier, annual in blocks2:
    print(f"  {tier}: ${annual}/mo annual")
PYEOF

# Get LastPass pricing from their business page
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/features/business" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, re
content = sys.stdin.read()
# Look for script tags with pricing data
scripts = re.findall(r'<script[^>]*>(.*?)</script>', content, re.DOTALL)
for i, s in enumerate(scripts):
    if 'price' in s.lower() or 'per user' in s.lower() or '\$' in s:
        # Extract relevant parts
        prices = re.findall(r'\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?', s)
        if prices:
            print(f'Script {i}: prices={prices[:10]}')
            print(s[:500])
            print('---')
" 2>/dev/null | head -60

# Try LastPass pricing page directly
curl -sL "https://www.lastpass.com/pricing" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, re
content = sys.stdin.read()
# Extract text content
text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', content)
text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
# Find pricing sections
for m in re.finditer(r'.{0,200}\$[\d,]+.{0,200}', text):
    print(m.group(0).strip()[:300])
" 2>/dev/null | head -30

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re

with open('/tmp/1password_pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Find Business tier pricing
business = re.findall(r'entryTitle["\']:\s*["\']PWM Pricing - Business.{0,500}', content)
for b in business[:3]:
    print("Business block:", b[:500])
    print("---")

# Find Enterprise tier
enterprise = re.findall(r'entryTitle["\']:\s*["\']PWM Pricing - Enterprise.{0,500}', content)
for e in enterprise[:3]:
    print("Enterprise block:", e[:500])
    print("---")

# Get Bitwarden pricing - try their API or structured data

print('Length:', len(text))
print(text[:2000])

# Try Bitwarden's web app pricing API

import sys, json
try:
    data = json.loads(sys.stdin.read())
    print(json.dumps(data, indent=2)[:3000])
except:
    print('Not JSON')
    print(sys.stdin.read()[:500])

content = sys.stdin.read()
# Look for any dollar amounts

print('Prices found:', set(prices))
# Look for tier names

print('Tiers found:', set(tiers))
# Look for per user

for p in per_user[:10]:
    print('  per user:', p.strip()[:120])
" 2>/dev/null

# Try Bitwarden's pricing from their web app's API with proper headers

try:
    data = json.loads(content)
    print(json.dumps(data, indent=2)[:5000])
except:
    print('Not JSON, length:', len(content))
    print(content[:500])
" 2>/dev/null
